## TSMC reports 34% Q2 revenue growth; analyst raises price target with highly bullish investment framing

34% Q2 revenue growth at the world's dominant foundry is a direct demand confirmation signal for AI compute. For names with leading-edge TSMC exposure — NVDA, AAPL, AMD, AVGO — this is a positive read-through on near-term capacity and pricing dynamics.

- Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) reported 34% revenue growth in Q2 year-over-year, per the headline.
- An analyst raised their price target following results, characterizing the investment case as 'highly bullish.'
- Results reinforce AI-driven foundry demand at the leading-edge layer.
- No specific revised price target level is confirmed in the available headline text.
